user_prompt:
Perfect! I've created a comprehensive followup prompt framework for the next Claude Code session to research all plugins and adapt them to the new autonomous system.

Summary
5 new prompt files created (1,631 lines total):

📋 README.md (301 lines)
Navigation guide pointing next session to all resources, context from this session, and how to use the prompts.

🎯 PLUGIN-RESEARCH-QUICKSTART.md (150+ lines)
TL;DR version: 5-minute read with 3 big questions, 6-hour timeline, pro tips.

📖 NEXT-SESSION-PLUGIN-RESEARCH.md (400+ lines)
Full detailed research plan with 5 phases:

Phase 1: Discovery (find all plugins)
Phase 2: Analysis (deep dive on top 10)
Phase 3: Classification (Tier 1/2/3 ranking)
Phase 4: Proof of Concept (build 1 working adapter)
Phase 5: Documentation (document pattern for others)
✅ PLUGIN-RESEARCH-CHECKLIST.md (450+ lines)
Step-by-step execution checklist with:

Pre-work checklist (5 min)
5 phases with checkpoints
Quality metrics
Troubleshooting guide
📝 PLUGIN-RESEARCH-TEMPLATE.md (500+ lines)
Reusable template for analyzing each plugin with 10 sections:

Identification, purpose, implementation, autonomous assessment, opportunity scoring, adaptation plan, code locations, notes, status, sign-off
What Next Session Should Do
Objectives (6 hours):

Discover 20+ plugins (create inventory)
Analyze 10+ plugins (deep dive)
Classify by tier (Tier 1 = must adapt, Tier 2 = should, Tier 3 = nice)
Adapt 1 plugin (proof-of-concept with working tests)
Document the replication pattern (how to adapt others)
Output: .claude/audit/, .claude/adapters/, .claude/doc/ directories with all findings, 1 working adapter, and a guide for replicating the pattern.

Starting Point for Next Session
Open .claude/prompts/README.md (5 min)
Open .claude/prompts/PLUGIN-RESEARCH-QUICKSTART.md (10 min)
Use .claude/prompts/PLUGIN-RESEARCH-CHECKLIST.md as execution guide
Copy .claude/prompts/PLUGIN-RESEARCH-TEMPLATE.md for each plugin analyzed
All files are committed and pushed. Framework is production-ready. 🚀
instruction: Interpret ALL literally - no partial work allowed
